claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To start the assessment, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for.

**First question:** How old is your child? 

The age matters because:
- Children **under 16** are generally eligible
- Young people aged **16-19** may be eligible if they're in approved education or training
- Those aged **20 or over** are not eligible for Child Benefit

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children, let me know about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! I'm inquiring about my son, Alex. He's 20 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. I understand you're inquiring about your son Alex, who is 20 years old and lives at home with you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but based on the Child Benefit rules, **you are not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Here's why:**

Child Benefit is only available for:
- Children **under 16**, or
- Young people aged **16-19** who are in approved education or training

Since Alex is 20 years old, he no longer qualifies as a "qualifying child" under the Child Benefit rules, even though he lives at home with you.

**Important to note:**
- If Alex recently turned 20, your Child Benefit would have stopped when he reached that age
- If you have any other children who are under 20 (and meet the other criteria), you may still be eligible for Child Benefit for them

Do you have any other children you'd like to check eligibility for, or do you have any questions about this decision?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I understand, thank you for explaining that to me. That's clear - since Alex is 20, he's too old for Child Benefit. I don't have any other children, so I suppose that's that then. I appreciate you taking the time to go through this with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: You're very welcome! I'm glad I could clarify the situation for you, even though the outcome wasn't what you might have hoped for.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Yes, that's correct. Since Alex is 20 and you don't have any other children, you're not eligible for Child Benefit at this time.

Just to recap:
- **Alex (20 years old)**: Not eligible - above the maximum age limit

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you have another child or become responsible for a younger child), you can always apply for Child Benefit at that time.

If you have any other questions about benefits or need guidance on other types of support that might be available to you, please don't hesitate to ask. Take care!

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old. Under UK Child Benefit rules, a qualifying child must be under 16 years old, or aged 16-19 and in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old, he does not meet the age requirements for Child Benefit, regardless of whether he lives at home or any other circumstances.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one son, Alex, who is 20 years old. Child Benefit is not available for children aged 20 or over. Therefore, the claimant is ineligible for Child Benefit.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.reason: No qualifying child under 16, or under 20 in approved education/training
response.guidance: Child must be under 16, or aged 16-19 and in approved education or training. If child just turned 20, benefit stops. If 16-17 year old leaves education, benefit can continue for 20 weeks if they register with careers service or armed services.

Since Alex is 20 years old, he does not meet the age requirements for Child Benefit, regardless of whether he lives at home or any other circumstances.
